title: Boucerosia gussoneana

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, fever, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Boucerosia gussoneana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.

This classification places it within the group of plants commonly known as the bushberry family. The Malpighiaceae family is further divided into several genera, with Boucerosia gussoneana being one of the species within this family.
